messages.content:
CW4Kids is a Saturday morning block on the CW Eon Kid † (May 24, 2008 - June 14, 2008) (TV-Y7-FV)
Johnny Test † (May 24, 2008 - June 14, 2008) (TV-Y7) (now shown on Cartoon Network)
World of Quest † (May 24, 2008 - June 14, 2008) (TV-Y7-FV)
The Batman † (June 21, 2008 - August 2, 2008) (TV-Y7-FV)
Tom and Jerry Tales † (May 24, 2008 - September 6, 2008) (TV-Y)
Yu-Gi-Oh! GX ‡ (June 21, 2008 - September 6, 2008) (TV-Y7-FV)
Magi-Nation † ‡ (May 24, 2008 - September 20, 2008) (TV-Y and TV-Y7)
Viva Piñata (September 13, 2008 - October 18, 2008) (TV-Y and TV-Y7)
Chaotic (June 21, 2008 - December 27, 2008) (TV-Y7-FV)
The Spectacular Spider-Man † (May 24, 2008 - January 31, 2009) (TV-Y7-FV)
Will and Dewitt † (May 24, 2008 - April 4, 2009) (TV-Y) (now shown on Cookie Jar Kids Network)
Kirby: Right Back at Ya! (June 6, 2009 - July 18, 2009) (TV-Y7-FV)
Chaotic: M’arrillian Invasion (January 3, 2009 - October 17, 2009) (TV-Y7-FV)
GoGoRiki (September 27, 2008 - November 14, 2009) (TV-Y and TV-Y7)
Huntik: Secrets & Seekers (January 3, 2009 - December 26, 2009) (TV-Y7-FV)
Kamen Rider: Dragon Knight (December 13, 2008 - December 26, 2009) (TV-Y7-FV)
Rollbots (September 19, 2009 - January 23, 2010) (TV-Y7-FV)
Skunk Fu! † (May 24, 2008 - October 18, 2008; May 29, 2010 - July 10, 2010) (TV-Y7)
Winx Club (July 18, 2009 - July 17, 2010) (TV-Y7-FV)
Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (June 21, 2008 - August 28, 2010) (TV-Y7-FV) (This includes the later retooled/renamed seasons)
† - Program transitioned from final schedule of Kids’ WB. Write a forum convo in May 2011 wondering what's the current 7am to 12pm lineup, and also 4 shows are back

User1: Hey CW4Kids enthusiasts! It's been quite a shift since the Kids’ WB days, hasn’t it? I'm trying to catch up on the current 7am to 12pm Saturday morning lineup as of May 2011. Does anyone know what's currently airing? Plus, I heard through the grapevine that 4 shows from the past have made a comeback. Which ones are those?

User2: Oh, definitely a shift! If I'm not mistaken, the lineup around that time has seen quite a mix. I don’t have the exact schedule but from memory, I believe "Yu-Gi-Oh!" and "Dragon Ball Z Kai" were in there. As for the comebacks, I'm pretty sure "Yu-Gi-Oh! 5D's" was one of them making a return. The details are a bit hazy on the rest. 

User3: Correct on "Yu-Gi-Oh! 5D's", User2. The CW4Kids/Toonzai block really loves their Yu-Gi-Oh! series. Another show that made a return was "Cubix: Robots for Everyone". It was one of those shows that transitioned from the Kids’ WB lineup. As for the full schedule as of May 2011, I’m drawing a blank. Surely, someone must keep track of these lineups?

User4: Hey all! You’re right about the returns. Adding to what’s been mentioned, "Sonic X" and "Tai Chi Chasers" were part of the lineup too. Sonic X coming back was a nice nostalgia trip for many. Though, I’m not 100% sure about "Tai Chi Chasers" being a comeback or a new addition at that point. The full 7am to 12pm lineup escapes me, but it was a blend of action and adventure with a nice mix of educational content sprinkled in.

User5: You guys are on the money. Also, can't forget about the educational content that complemented the action-oriented shows. "Rescue Heroes" was part of that effort to mix things up a bit, catering to a wider range of interests and ages. It's actually quite fascinating seeing how CW4Kids evolved its programming over the years, blending entertainment with learning. Does anyone know if there were any specific themes or special programming blocks during this time?

User6: Spot on, everyone. CW4Kids/Toonzai was making an effort to balance their lineup with both entertainment and educational content. Besides the individual shows, there were thematic blocks occasionally, especially around holidays or special events, featuring episodes from different shows that fit a specific theme - could be anything from friendship, adventure, to environmental conservation. It was their way of making Saturday mornings both fun and somewhat educational. However, specifics on the themes used in May 2011 elude me. The landscape of Saturday morning cartoons has indeed changed but, for many of us, remains a cherished memory.
Write a follow up asking if Gogoriki is back

User1: Thanks for shedding light on the lineup and the comebacks, folks! This conversation brings back so many great memories of tuning in every Saturday morning. Given the buzz around shows making a return, does anyone know if "GoGoRiki" has made a comeback as well? I remember it having a pretty unique animation style and some quirky characters. Would love to see it back on the airwaves!
Write a follow up where a user provides the full 7am to 12pm lineup

User7: Hey User1, and everyone else curious about the CW4Kids lineup! Managed to dig up some info and here’s a more detailed look at the full 7am to 12pm Saturday morning lineup as of May 2011 that you were asking about. Although I couldn't find any confirmation on "GoGoRiki" making a return by that time, here's what was airing:

7:00 AM – "Cubix: Robots for Everyone": Starting the morning with our beloved robots.
7:30 AM – "Sonic X": Speeding into action with Sonic and his friends.
8:00 AM – "Yu-Gi-Oh! 5D's": Keeping the card duels alive in the morning.
8:30 AM – "Dragon Ball Z Kai": Bringing intense action and the adventures of Goku and the Z Warriors.
9:00 AM – "Tai Chi Chasers": Adding some magical adventures into the mix.
9:30 AM – "Yu-Gi-Oh! ZEXAL": Another installment in the Yu-Gi-Oh series, featuring new characters and duels.
10:00 AM – "Magi-Nation": Continuing the adventure with magical battles and quests.
10:30 AM – "Dinosaur King": Mixing dinosaurs and cards into one entertaining show.
11:00 AM – "Rescue Heroes": Shifting gears towards more educational and inspirational content.
11:30 AM – "The Spectacular Spider-Man": Ending the lineup with web-slinging action to keep viewers engaged.

This lineup brought a good mix of action, adventure, and educational content to Saturday mornings, ensuring there was something for everyone. As for "GoGoRiki", it seems it wasn't part of the lineup during this period, but who knows about future programming decisions! The nostalgia is real though; makes you miss the simplicity of those days, doesn’t it?
Write a follow up in May 2013 (in this AU) with the 7am to 12pm lineup used then

User8: Time really flies, doesn't it? Here we are in May 2013, and the CW4Kids/Toonzai lineup has shuffled once again. For those wondering about the current 7am to 12pm Saturday morning slots, I've got you covered. Interestingly, it seems there have been quite a few changes since 2011. Unfortunately, for those hoping for "GoGoRiki" news, it doesn't appear to have made a return in this lineup either. But let's look at what's currently on:

7:00 AM – "Rescue Heroes": Still starting the morning with our heroes, teaching the values of teamwork and bravery.
7:30 AM – "Power Rangers Megaforce": Bringing action and adventure early in the morning with the newest team of Rangers.
8:00 AM – "Yu-Gi-Oh! ZEXAL": Continuing the adventures from the last timeline with new duels and challenges.
8:30 AM – "Iron Man: Armored Adventures": Introducing some superhero action into the mix with young Tony Stark.
9:00 AM – "Justice League Unlimited": Expanding the action with a larger roster of heroes battling against evil.
9:30 AM – "Batman: The Brave and the Bold": Keeping the superhero theme going with Batman teaming up with various heroes from the DC Universe.
10:00 AM – "Dragon Ball Z Kai": This one's a holdover, still bringing epic battles and the story of Goku and friends.
10:30 AM – "Sonic X": Back again, zipping through challenges at the speed of sound.
11:00 AM – "The Spectacular Spider-Man": Another returning show, spinning webs and catching villains.
11:30 AM – "Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles": Ending the block with the heroes in a half-shell tackling evil and eating pizza.

Quite the blend of superheroes, action-adventure, and a touch of educational content with "Rescue Heroes" holding its spot. This lineup feels like a solid nod to classic heroes with a modern twist, perfect for Saturday morning excitement. What does everyone think of the changes? Any surprises or disappointments in the lineup for you?
Write a follow up convo where a user is beginning to lose hope it will ever come back

User9: Wow, seeing the evolution of the CW4Kids/Toonzai lineup over the years really brings back memories. But I have to admit, every time we discuss the current lineup, I secretly hope to see "GoGoRiki" make a surprise comeback. It's 2013 now, and with each update, that hope dims a bit more. The show had such a unique charm and humor, not to mention the distinctive animation style that set it apart from everything else on the block. It's a shame newer audiences might not get to experience it on Saturday mornings like we did.

I understand that programming needs to evolve with its audience and that nostalgia can't drive an entire schedule, but it's hard not to feel a bit disappointed. The current lineup is solid, and I can see the appeal of the superhero and action-adventure themes they're banking on – it's a formula that works. Yet, there was something special about "GoGoRiki" that offered a different flavor, something a bit offbeat compared to the usual fare.

Does anyone else feel this way, or am I holding onto a piece of the past too tightly? Maybe it's time to let go and appreciate the classics through reruns and online platforms, but a part of me will always hold out a bit of hope for its return to the CW4Kids/Toonzai block.
Write a forum convo in November 2014 where other users console him, and also a user kindly provides the lineup in use then

User10: Hey User9, I totally get where you're coming from. "GoGoRiki" really was a one-of-a-kind show that brought something different to the table. It's hard to see our favorite shows not getting the recognition or the reruns they deserve. But you know, the great thing about being a fan these days is that there are more ways than ever to revisit the shows we miss. Maybe there's a streaming service out there with "GoGoRiki" in its library, or possibly DVDs? The community here is always amazing at digging up resources like that.

User11: Absolutely agree with User10. User9, it's totally normal to feel nostalgic about these shows. They are a part of our childhood and have shaped our tastes in entertainment. "GoGoRiki" had a unique essence, and it's understandable to miss its presence in the current lineup. Plus, the animation industry is always full of surprises - who knows, maybe one day we'll get a reboot or a special airing. Keep the hope alive, but also keep enjoying what's currently available and discovering new favorites!

User12: It's nice to see such support here. On that note, for everyone's curiosity, here's the CW4Kids/Toonzai lineup as of November 2014, just to keep everyone in the loop:

7:00 AM – "Digimon Fusion": Starting the day with digital monsters and adventures.
7:30 AM – "Sonic Boom": The newest iteration of our favorite speedy hedgehog.
8:00 AM – "Yu-Gi-Oh! ZEXAL": Continuing the card-dueling saga with even more intense battles.
8:30 AM – "Pac-Man and the Ghostly Adventures": Bringing a classic video game character to Saturday mornings.
9:00 AM – "Teen Titans Go!": The lighter, comedic take on our beloved Teen Titans.
9:30 AM – "The Spectacular Spider-Man": Keeping the web-slinging action alive.
10:00 AM – "Justice League Unlimited": More from the DC Universe's greatest heroes.
10:30 AM – "The Avengers: Earth's Mightiest Heroes": Marvel's finest joining the action-packed lineup.
11:00 AM – "Pokémon": The adventures of Ash and Pikachu continue.
11:30 AM – "Beyblade: Shogun Steel": Ending the morning with epic Beyblade battles.

User13: Thanks for the update, User12! And User9, don't lose hope. Shows come and go, but the memories and joy they bring us stay forever. Plus, the animation world is small, and you never know when a nostalgic wave might bring back our favorite shows in one form or another. Till then, let's keep sharing our memories and enjoying the new adventures Saturday mornings bring us!
Write a follow up where a user says "Come to think of it, 4Kids haven't programmed the block since January 2013" and then says WB are programming it again, 4Kids paid to keep their name

User14: Come to think of it, 4Kids haven't actually programmed the block since January 2013. It's been a while, hasn't it? The nostalgia keeps us referring back to it as the CW4Kids/Toonzai, but that's not entirely accurate anymore. The mantle was passed back to WB, and they've been curating the lineup since then. Despite the change in programming hands, 4Kids negotiated to keep their name associated with the block, which has definitely kept the brand alive in our conversations and memories.

It's interesting to see how the block has evolved over time, transitioning between these major players in the animation and children's programming industry. WB's return to programming it again has introduced a different flavor to the lineup, as we can see from the mix of shows currently airing. Yet, the essence of Saturday morning cartoons, that excitement of catching your favorite show with your morning cereal, remains the same.

Given this shift, it's fascinating how the legacy of 4Kids still looms large over the block, influencing how we discuss and remember it, even as WB steers the programming in new directions. The brand and its influence on a generation of viewers are a testament to the impact 4Kids had during its tenure.

Write a convo asking if the 4Kids TV split screen credits stylization is still here, and a user says the credits stylization has reverted too, also promos use CN announcers, The only thing linking it to 4Kids is some of the programming and the name "CW4Kids", Also a user says the 3 most popular shows

User15: Does anyone know if the 4Kids TV split screen credits stylization is still a thing? I always thought that was a cool way to keep us engaged while rolling the credits. Plus, it was a signature move that stood out. With all the changes happening, I wonder if that's something that's been kept or if it's been phased out?

User16: Actually, the credits stylization has reverted back as well, along with several other changes since WB took over the programming. The split screen credits were really unique to 4Kids, offering content and promos on one side while the credits rolled on the other. Now, it's more traditional, straightforward credits at the end of shows. Even the promos and announcements have seen a change, with CN (Cartoon Network) announcers being featured, which definitely gives it a different vibe.

The only real link to 4Kids at this point is some of the programming that has carried over and, of course, the name "CW4Kids" being retained, likely for brand recognition and nostalgia's sake. It's an interesting blend of the old and the new, but the essence of the programming block certainly feels distinct from the 4Kids era.

User17: Speaking of programming, even with changes, there are definitely standout shows that have become the face of the current lineup. The three most popular shows right now would likely be "Pokémon", "Yu-Gi-Oh! ZEXAL", and "Teen Titans Go!". Pokémon and Yu-Gi-Oh! have been staples for years, continuing to draw in viewers with their latest series and seasons, and "Teen Titans Go!" brings a fresh, comedic spin to the superhero genre that's been resonating well with the audience. These shows have managed to capture the essence of engaging, adventurous, and often humorous storytelling that keeps the spirit of Saturday morning cartoons alive.
Write a convo asking if it feels more aesthetically like the 2008 CW4Kids or the 2005 Kids WB in terms of stylization, bumpers, etc

User18: With all these changes going on with the CW4Kids block, I’ve been wondering if the current vibe and aesthetics lean more towards the 2008 CW4Kids look and feel, or if it’s veering back towards the 2005 Kids' WB era, especially in terms of stylization, bumpers, etc. Has anyone else felt a particular shift or theme reminiscent of one or the other?

User19: That’s a great question! From what I’ve noticed, it feels like there’s been a deliberate shift back towards the kind of aesthetic and energy we saw with Kids' WB in 2005. The bumpers, in particular, seem to channel more of that early-to-mid-2000s vibe, which is quite nostalgic. It's less about the flashy, digitalized feel that was signature of 4Kids in 2008, and more about vibrant, dynamic animations that mirror the Kids' WB style.

User20: I've felt that too, User19. The overall presentation has taken on a lighter, more playful approach reminiscent of Kids' WB. It’s almost as if they’re trying to recapture that Saturday morning excitement with cartoons vibe that was such a staple back in the day. Though the programming has evolved, the interstitial content, like bumpers and promos, really does throw back to the earlier days rather than sticking to the more polished, sleek look that defined the CW4Kids era.

User21: Adding to what's been said, even the choice of shows seems to reflect this nostalgic nod. While the inclusion of newer series is a must for any programming block aiming to stay relevant, the way they're presented, coupled with the sequence of classic shows, offers a blend that tips the hat to the 2005 era. It’s interesting to see this retro approach being embraced, especially when everything else in media seems to be pushing towards the ultra-modern.

User22: Totally agreeing with everyone here. It’s like a trip down memory lane, but with a modern twist. The use of CN announcers for promos, as mentioned earlier, even adds to that blend of old and new by bringing in a familiar voice from another golden era of cartoons. The CW4Kids name might still be up there, but the spirit of Kids' WB is alive and well in the aesthetics and stylization, making it feel like a true spiritual successor to those unforgettable Saturday morning lineups.

Write a convo where the logo is even a anthropomorphic, rubbery version of the logo again, Also a user asks when the KWB style presentation was relaunched, and another user said it happened throughout 2013

User23: Has anyone else noticed that even the logo has gone full circle back to being an anthropomorphic, rubbery version reminiscent of the early days? It's like seeing an old friend come back to life in a new era. The animation and personality it brings to the screen definitely set the tone for the whole block. It's fascinating how they're embracing the nostalgia while still pushing forward.

User24: Yeah, the logo adds such a unique character to the whole presentation! It really stands out and brings a sense of fun and flexibility that seems to say the block doesn't take itself too seriously. It's all about the excitement and adventure of cartoons. When exactly did they decide to relaunch the KWB (Kids' WB) style presentation? It seems like they've been gradually transitioning, but I can't pinpoint when it all started.

User25: The shift back towards the Kids' WB style in presentation, including the return of the anthropomorphic, lively logo, actually happened throughout 2013. It was a gradual transition, with various elements being reintroduced or revamped to capture that early 2000s essence. By embracing this nostalgia, the block has managed to reignite interest and bring back viewers who grew up with Kids' WB, while also attracting a new generation. It’s been an interesting blend of the old and new, showing a deep appreciation for the block’s history.
